title: Asuka Telecaster
wikitext:
[]: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by EvaChero » Mon May 17, 2021 3:50 pm has anybody seen this before? wow i'd love to have one but holy hossenphepher the prices! I'd be afraid to play it! https://www.ebay.com/sch/i.html?_from=R ... r&_sacat=0

[]: Re: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by GendoBus » Tue May 18, 2021 5:06 pm The idea of making a guitar about evangelion is great, but I think that the stratocaster fits better for Asuka and I don't really like it, aside from the nerv steel neck plate, there isn't anything that tells you that is an evangelion theme guitar. I'd love to have it though, just because is an evangelion guitar ahaha

[]: Re: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by EvaChero » Wed May 19, 2021 9:26 am I have to agree that design-wise it's a bit underwhelming, it's mostly a color scheme. Those appear to be "aluma-tone" pickups which is an interesting departure.

[]: Re: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by GendoBus » Wed May 19, 2021 6:03 pm Yeah and why does the pickguard have a green part? I mean the typical Asuka colors are red and black, it seems that the design wasn't really well thought. I have never heard about this kind of pick-ups, but I've searched them and it seems they are like an another take of the noiseless pickups with an higher output, have you ever tried them?

[]: Re: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by EvaChero » Thu May 20, 2021 9:04 am Have thought about it...they look pretty kool but my understanding is the perceived output is different enough from a standard pickup that you can't mix them with standard pickups unless you like doing sound adjustments on the fly. I understand they are incredibly clear and noiseless. Not the cheapest pickup either but I'd like to experiment with a set maybe on a cheap beater guitar. Its a project for a later time for me. I think the green on the pickguard may represent Unit 02's eyes.

[]: Re: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by GendoBus » Mon May 24, 2021 4:27 am Ah I see, they are interesting but I can't see someone who can benefit from this type pickup, I mean the single-coils make some noise, but at the end of the day it's what that makes them and if someone is using a good set of single coils the hum is tolerable. Returning on the Asuka telecaster, they could've made the volume and tone knobs green to mimic the green eyes of Unit-02.

[]: Re: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by EvaChero » Mon May 24, 2021 10:16 am GendoBus wrote: Ah I see, they are interesting but I can't see someone who can benefit from this type pickup, I mean the single-coils make some noise, but at the end of the day it's what that makes them and if someone is using a good set of single coils the hum is tolerable. Returning on the Asuka telecaster, they could've made the volume and tone knobs green to mimic the green eyes of Unit-02.

[]: Re: Asuka Telecaster             Quote Post by GendoBus » Fri May 28, 2021 4:07 pm The Asuka tele could have been a kewl item and could have been affordable but these appear more like lazy cash grabs than anything worthwhile.
